name: Firefighters First
description_short: Member-owned credit union founded by firefighters, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards exclusively to firefighters and their families.
description_long:
Firefighters First Credit Union (FFCU) is a specialized credit union established by and for members of the firefighting community. The organization explicitly states its mission as 'To Improve the Financial Lives of Our Fire Family' and maintains a membership model restricted to firefighters and their families. The credit union operates a full-service banking platform with online access and customer support available daily from 7am-8pm.

FFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts (checking, savings, share certificates), consumer loans (vehicle loans, RV/boat loans, personal loans), mortgage services with unique features, and credit card products with cashback rewards. Their vehicle loan rates start at 4.49% APR, RV/boat loans at 5.99% APR, and they feature a 12-month share certificate at 3.80% APY. The mortgage product includes an innovative 'Move & Save' feature allowing members to carry their current mortgage rate to a new home for three years.

What distinguishes FFCU is its specialized focus on the firefighter community and commitment to member education and wellbeing. The organization partners with the Fire Family Foundation to support firefighter mental health initiatives and family services. They provide extensive financial education resources including Financial Fitness tools, a 'Fire Kids' program for financial parenting, and regular blog content. Customer service emphasizes personalized support through video chat consultation and direct CEO contact options for member feedback.

As a credit union serving a specific occupational group, FFCU benefits from member loyalty and community alignment but is limited to those with firefighting eligibility. Their rates are competitive but not exceptionally lowest-market, and membership restrictions mean this is not suitable for the general population seeking basic banking services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Home mortgages with rate portability feature
Member quick request and feedback system
pros: Specialized focus on firefighter community with aligned mission reduces conflicts of interest and builds trust
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.49% APR and 12-month CDs at 3.80% APY
Unique 'Move & Save' mortgage feature allowing rate portability for 3 years to new homes
Unlimited cashback rewards credit card with up to 2% cash back on all purchases
Extensive financial education resources including Financial Fitness tools and Fire Kids program
Extended customer support hours (7am-8pm daily) with video chat and personalized service options
Commitment to firefighter family mental health through Fire Family Foundation partnerships
cons: Membership restricted to firefighters and families, excluding general population
Limited information about checking account fees, minimum balances, or overdraft policies on website
No indication of whether they offer business checking beyond business loan products
Rates, while competitive, are not positioned as best-in-market for any product category
Website content is sparse on loan approval timelines and specific eligibility requirements beyond membership
best_for: Active firefighters seeking community-aligned banking with personalized service
Firefighter families wanting to bundle multiple financial products with one trusted institution
First-time homebuyers in the firefighting profession interested in flexible mortgage options
Firefighters financing vehicles or RVs seeking competitive rates with member-focused support
diagnosis: Firefighters First Credit Union is ideal for firefighters and their immediate families seeking full-service banking with community values alignment and personalized service. The primary caveat is membership eligibility restriction—those outside the firefighting profession cannot join—and the website lacks transparency on certain account features like fees and minimum balances that should be verified before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Member-owned credit union founded by firefighters, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards to firefighters and families.
